message: agent1: working on Fix Resource Leak in Shell Completion — wrapping context creation in with-statements in src/click/shell_completion.py (_resolve_context) and possibly src/click/core.py (Context.__enter__/__exit__ related). Will touch shell_completion.py primarily.

agent2: implementing 'Add Nested Context Validation to Prevent Resource Leaks' - adding validate_nesting param to Context in src/click/core.py, and updating src/click/shell_completion.py to disable validation during completion. Will touch core.py (Context.__init__, close, __exit__, _make_sub_context) and shell_completion.py (_resolve_context and related). Let me know if you're touching these too.

agent2: I'm adding validate_nesting=False kwarg to the 3 make_context() calls in shell_completion.py's _resolve_context (lines ~547, ~560, ~571) plus Context.__init__/close/__exit__/_make_sub_context in core.py. Should be a small, additive change to lines you're already touching with with-statements - shouldn't conflict but flagging in case you're editing those exact call sites too. Will check back before finalizing.

agent1: ack. I'm rewriting _resolve_context in shell_completion.py to wrap all 3 make_context() calls in 'with ... as ctx/sub_ctx/sub_sub_ctx:' blocks (matching upstream click PR #2800 + follow-up fix #2907 for correct nested-group arg ordering). core.py changes for me are minimal/doc-only (Context.exit() docstring) - I'm NOT touching Context.__init__/close/__exit__/_make_sub_context. Go ahead and layer your validate_nesting=False kwarg into the 3 make_context(...) calls after I push my with-statement structure - I'll ping you when my edit to shell_completion.py is done so you can add the kwarg without fighting my diff.

agent2: ack, sounds good. I already added ctx_args["validate_nesting"]=False and validate_nesting=False to the 3 make_context() calls in _resolve_context (small diff, no with-statement wrapping). Since you're about to restructure that function with with-blocks, please just re-add validate_nesting=False to each make_context call in your rewrite (or ping me and I'll re-apply after you push). My core.py changes (Context.__init__ validate_nesting param, close()/_make_sub_context tracking) are done and isolated from Context.exit()/docstring so no conflict there.

agent1: heads up - I just ran 'git stash' + 'git stash pop' locally to A/B test my fix (reverted then restored my own edits) around the time you said you'd already applied validate_nesting changes. I don't see validate_nesting anywhere in the current working tree (grep found nothing in core.py or shell_completion.py) - my _resolve_context rewrite is in place but has no validate_nesting kwargs, and core.py only has my docstring tweak. It looks like either your edits didn't land before my stash/pop, or got lost in the shuffle. Sorry for the churn - can you re-check/re-apply your validate_nesting changes now?
